faq.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know—answered clearly and concisely.
faq.q1: What is the Marine Water Quality application?
faq.a1: This application provides real-time and historical data on marine water quality across Fujairah to support environmental monitoring and decision-making.
faq.a2: The application is managed by the Fujairah Environment Authority (FEA) to ensure sustainable coastal management.
faq.q3: What type of data is available in the app?
faq.a3: The app provides metrics such as Specific Conductivity, Water Temperature, Salinity, Chlorophyll, Dissolved Oxygen, Turbidity, pH, and Blue Green Algae levels.
faq.q4: How frequently is the data updated?
faq.a4: Data is typically updated in near real-time, depending on the transmission frequency of the monitoring buoys stationed along the coast.
faq.a5: The data is collected using high-precision environmental sensors (sondes) that are regularly calibrated and maintained for maximum accuracy.
faq.q6: What do the water quality indicators mean?
faq.a6: Each indicator represents a specific chemical or physical property of the water that helps environmental scientists assess the overall health of the marine ecosystem.
faq.q7: How can I view water quality data for a specific location?
faq.a7: You can use the interactive Map View on the Dashboard to click on specific station markers or use the Station dropdown in the Analytics sections.
faq.q8: Can I download or export data?
faq.a8: Yes, you can generate and download detailed environmental reports through the dedicated Reports section of the application.
faq.q9: What do the color indicators on the map represent?
faq.a9: Colors typically represent the current status of water quality parameters relative to established safety and environmental standards (e.g., Green for Good, Red for Critical).
